title: So much shit to do, that we do none — title-as-aphorism
html:
TL;DR

  • Empty-body Apple Note from October 6, 2019 — the title IS the note form: "So much shit to do, that we do none??" The question marks are load-bearing.
  • Names the paralysis by stack failure-mode 16-year-old Yahir is observing in himself — the of unfinished tasks producing zero motion.
  • Sits one week before "Square your shit" (Oct 14, 2019) — same teen, same week, same diagnosis-then-prescription pair. Read together they are a self-coaching loop: name the failure-mode → issue the corrective.
  • Earliest extant articulation in the vault of what becomes the rut of complacency frame and the max effort commit response — Yahir's lifelong tension between seeing the stack and moving the stack.
  • Anchors the late-2019 GP teen era cluster of one-line self-talk notes (alongside the "You're ready, but know you can make mistakes" and "Square your shit" siblings).

Note

Eight words and two question marks. Yahir at 16 doing the note as letter to self move at its most compressed: title a note with the diagnosis, save it, walk away. The note is its own evidence — here is more shit to do (a note to write) and I did almost none of it (no body). The form enacts the content. This is the 's purest specimen — capture without follow-through, the very pattern the title indicts.

The pair with "Square your shit" eight days later turns this from a one-off into a method: name the slack, then issue the order. Both notes recur in spirit across the fear of not doing thread that runs from teen-Yahir through to the Austin thesis no-runway-discipline posture.

Connections

  • 2019 10 14 square your shit — the corrective that follows
  • fear of not doing — diagnosis names this failure-mode
  • — the form the note critiques
  • rut of complacency — the steady-state version of the same pattern
  • max effort commit — the long-term antidote

related.label: What's left — two-line self-prescription on the Fallen day
